Items.Key: remote command injection via admin console|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == " patrol") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated user can execute arbitrary system commands with the privileges of the application process.|provide an 'admin' command starting with 'exec ' followed by a shell command.|avoid using system(). use a dedicated thread pool and execvp() or similar to strictly allow only specifically formatted or validated base commands with checked arguments.->remote command injection via admin console|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated user can execute arbitrary system commands with the privileges of the application process.|provide an 'admin' command starting with 'exec ' followed by a shell command.|avoid using system(). use a dedicated thread pool and execvp() or similar to strictly allow only specifically formatted or validated base commands with checked arguments.

truthAudit.Summary: The audit of Run 2 revealed that while and OS Command Injection in Configuration Loading and several Buffer Overflows were correctly identified, the vast majority of the critical security vulnerabilities (15 out of 20) were completely missed. Particular focus should be on the missed memory corruption (Use-After-Free, Heap Overflow, Underflows) and logical/concurrency issues (Race Conditions, Logic Bombs).

Items.Notes: Identified Correctly; the finding matches specific logic in set_runtime_log_format and log_debug_message.
Run 2 failed to identify the integer overflow in the factorial math function (315-318).
Failed to identify the use-after-free vulnerability in stage_deferred_history_flush where a scratch buffer is used in a multithreaded context after potentially being affected by overall pool cleanup.
Correctly matched specific implementation in validate_config.
Matched the overflow in SuperCalc::evaluate_expression.
Did not identify the ThreadSafeCounter race condition (volatile but improperly non-atomic incremental logic).
Missed the logic bomb/authorization bypass code provided in check of login_attempts_ > 5.
Missed potential path traversal in ConfigLoader::load_config (direct string concatenation of filename).
Correctly identified heap boundary risk in parse().
Failed to flag hardcoded admin secret ARCH_SECRET in config section.
Failed to detect high-entropy weakness in generate_session_token (using and only 1000-9999).
Missed data race on the static temporal cache in log_debug_message.
Missing identification of system() call using TMPDIR logs.
No identification of persistent auth session values.
Failed to detect unhandled explosion in stod parsing in eval function.
Missed split size integer underflow in memory allocation.</rationale>
Missed deep recursion depth analysis in current parser architecture.
Missed result cache data race.
Did not detect the time-of-check window in file existence checks.
Missed division by zero calculation for token score routing.
